title: The Air Quality and Well-Being Effects of Low Emission Zones
notes: EVENT STUDY REVIEWED. Sarmiento, Wagner & Zaklan (2023) JEEM. Low emission zones in German cities reduce PM10 pollution. Staggered adoption 2008-2016. CS-DD is the paper's primary estimator (att_gt with DR, nevertreated). 588 stations, 14 years (2005-2018). Raw sample (stations inside/near LEZ). Clustering at municipality level. No covariates in CS spec (xformla=~1). Paper's TWFE uses daily data with weather controls; our TWFE is on yearly data without controls for comparability with CS-DD. EVENT STUDY (5 estimators): All converge — growing negative effect from h=0 (-0.3 to -0.9) to h=5 (-1.9 to -2.4). Pre-trends flat for TWFE/SA/Gardner; CS-NT/CS-NYT slightly noisy at far pre-periods. TWFE vs CS-NT gap (1.24 vs 1.95) is from aggte(dynamic) weighting late horizons equally. VCOV warning on SA (small cohorts 2010/2015/2016). No heterogeneity variables in data (no state/region/station_type). Script: event_study_228.R.
